name: Imam Sajjad (AS)
title: Zayn al-Abidin
shortDescription: The noble son of Imam Hussain (AS) who, by divine will, was severely ill on Ashura so the line of Imamate would not be severed.
fullStory:
Ali ibn al-Hussain (AS), known as Zayn al-Abidin and Sayyid al-Sajidin, is the fourth Imam of the Shia and the son of Imam Hussain (AS). He was about 23 years old during the event of Karbala, and his presence in this epic is considered a turning point in Shia history.

On the day of Ashura, by divine will, Imam Sajjad (AS) was severely ill and unable to wield a sword. This providential illness protected him from being killed in the battlefield, ensuring the line of Imamate would not be severed after the Master of Martyrs (AS). Nevertheless, he repeatedly tried to go to the battlefield, but Imam Hussain (AS) and Lady Zaynab (SA) prevented him.

After the martyrdom of Imam Hussain (AS) and the looting of the tents, the difficult period of captivity began. Despite his illness and heavy chains, Imam Sajjad (AS) took on the leadership of the caravan of captives. His fiery and crushing sermons in Kufa and Sham shook the foundations of Yazid's rule.

In the Umayyad Mosque of Sham, by introducing himself and the Prophet's (PBUH) family, Imam Sajjad (AS) exposed Yazid's true face and showed that the true victors of Karbala were the Ahl al-Bayt (AS).

After returning to Medina, he led the Imamate for 34 years. Through his continuous weeping for the Master of Martyrs (AS) and in the form of the supplications in Al-Sahifa Al-Sajjadiyya, he kept the message of Ashura and pure Islamic teachings alive in history forever.
quoteArabic: أَنا ابْنُ مَكَّةَ وَمِنى، أَنا ابْنُ زَمْزَمَ وَالصَّفا...
quote: (In the sermon of Sham:) I am the son of Mecca and Mina, I am the son of Zamzam and Safa...
